Usage: nexus [options] [command]

Nexus AI CLI - WordPress site management with AI superpowers

Options:
  -V, --version           output the version number
  -h, --help              display help for command

Commands:
  sites                   Manage Local and WPE sites
  wp                      Run WP-CLI commands on sites
  sync                    Sync sites between local and WPE
  wpe                     Manage WP Engine accounts and installs
  blueprints              Manage Local blueprints
  update [options]        Update the CLI to the latest version

  audit                   Site and plugin audits
  gateway                 AI gateway usage and cost reporting

content.content:
Usage: nexus content [options] [command]

Content and context management

Options:
  -h, --help                         display help for command

Commands:
  search [options] <target> <query>  Search content within a site
  search-all [options] <query>       Search content across all sites
  structure [options] <target>       Get site file structure
  index-status [options] <target>    Get indexing status for a site
  list-indexed [options]             List all indexed sites
  index <target>                     Index content for a site (creates
                                     searchable database of posts, pages, and
                                     products)
  reindex <target>                   [Deprecated] Use "nexus content index"
                                     instead
  help [command]                     display help for command

content.text:
Here's what I found across your local sites:

---

## WooCommerce / E-commerce Content

### Sites with actual WooCommerce content (posts/pages with real text):

**Faker Incorporated** — the richest match, with dedicated WooCommerce content:
- **"How to Get More Out of Your WooCommerce Store"** (post, score 0.52) — article about e-commerce optimization and squeezing more out of WooCommerce stores
- **"Services"** (page, score 0.52) — mentions digital strategy and e-commerce in the service portfolio
- **"Shop"** page + **"Cart"** page — WooCommerce storefront pages

**The Curated Shelf** — has a **Shop** page (WooCommerce store)

**faker-incorporated** — has **Shop** and **Cart** pages (separate from "Faker Incorporated" above — likely a different local site with a similar theme)

**nexustestsite1** and **nexus-test-site** — both have **Shop** pages

---

### Large number of WPE-pulled sites:
Many of your WPE-synced sites (20+) have WooCommerce's standard `[woocommerce_my_account]` and `[woocommerce_cart]` shortcode pages — these are WooCommerce's default pages auto-created on install, not unique content.

---

**Bottom line:** If you're looking for sites with meaningful WooCommerce content, **Faker Incorporated** is the one to focus on — it has an actual blog post about WooCommerce optimization. The WPE-prefixed sites all have WooCommerce installed but only the boilerplate pages.

Here's what I found across your local sites:

---

## WooCommerce / E-commerce Content

### Sites with actual WooCommerce content (posts/pages with real text):

**Faker Incorporated** — the richest match, with dedicated WooCommerce content:
- **"How to Get More Out of Your WooCommerce Store"** (post, score 0.52) — article about e-commerce optimization and squeezing more out of WooCommerce stores
- **"Services"** (page, score 0.52) — mentions digital strategy and e-commerce in the service portfolio
- **"Shop"** page + **"Cart"** page — WooCommerce storefront pages

**The Curated Shelf** — has a **Shop** page (WooCommerce store)

**faker-incorporated** — has **Shop** and **Cart** pages (separate from "Faker Incorporated" above — likely a different local site with a similar theme)

**nexustestsite1** and **nexus-test-site** — both have **Shop** pages

---

### Large number of WPE-pulled sites:
Many of your WPE-synced sites (20+) have WooCommerce's standard `[woocommerce_my_account]` and `[woocommerce_cart]` shortcode pages — these are WooCommerce's default pages auto-created on install, not unique content.

---

**Bottom line:** If you're looking for sites with meaningful WooCommerce content, **Faker Incorporated** is the one to focus on — it has an actual blog post about WooCommerce optimization. The WPE-prefixed sites all have WooCommerce installed but only the boilerplate pages.
